title: MemoryDb is a god object — 30+ methods across 8 unrelated concerns in one struct
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
solid
## Location
src/memory.rs:122-1337 (impl MemoryDb)
## Violation
A single struct exposes 30+ public methods covering eight orthogonal subsystems:

- Archival memory (memory_save / search / get / update / delete / list / stats / clear)
- Core memory (get / get_section / update / format)
- Schema migration (ensure_schema_on, run_migrations_on, migrate_v1_on, migrate_v2_on, migrate_v3_on)
- Short-term sessions (save_session_summary, get_recent_sessions, format_recent_context_for_prompt)
- Activity log (log_activity, get_session_activities, get_session_files_modified, get_session_issues)
- Coding patterns (save_coding_pattern, get_patterns_for_file)
- File relationships (save_file_relationship, get_related_files)
- Error patterns + learned preferences (save_error_pattern, resolve_error_pattern, save_learned_preference, get_all_preferences)
- Raw SQL escape hatch (execute_raw)

The file is 1767 lines, the impl block is contiguous, and every method takes &self — all eight subsystems share one Mutex<Connection>, so any slow query (e.g. format_file_knowledge running three joins) blocks unrelated paths. SRP says "one reason to change" — this struct has eight. Adding a new memory kind currently requires editing this file, a new test mod, the migration ladder, and the three formatting helpers.
## Mandated refactor
1. Extract per-concern repositories behind narrow traits: ArchivalRepo, CoreMemoryRepo, SessionRepo, ActivityRepo, PatternRepo, RelationshipRepo, ErrorRepo, PreferenceRepo (ISP — clients depend on the slice they use).
2. Move schema migration into its own schema module, callable from MemoryDb::open.
3. Replace the prompt-formatting methods (format_core_memory_for_prompt, format_recent_context_for_prompt, format_file_knowledge, format_learned_preferences) with a PromptFormatter that depends on the read-side traits — keeps the LLM-output sink out of the SQLite layer.
4. Connection-sharing concern: wrap connection in Arc<Mutex<Connection>>, hand a clone to each repo. Or migrate to r2d2 pool for true parallelism.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
